zh.Cornhill-Upon-Tweed, Northumberland, Eng: 英國英格蘭諾圖波藍德可恩爾 奧 特維德
zh.lifeSummary: 當Gervase de Cornhill於1123年出生在英國諾圖波藍德可恩爾 奧 特維德時，他的父親Roger de Cornhill, Joint Sheriff of London54歲，母親Ingenolda de Conteville29歲。他和Agnes Cornhill至少有4個兒子和1個女兒。他於1170年被列為royal justice。他去世於1183年享年60歲。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Cornhill
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: 1  English: locative name in SE England mainly from Cornhill in London but elsewhere alternatively from Cornhill (Northumb) or one of the many minor places so called. It has often become   Cornell   and has sometimes been confused with   Cornwall  , and   Cornwell  , as in the case of Thomas Cornell, 1722 in   Parish Registers   (Horringer, Suffolk), who is also called Cornwall (1736), Cornwell (1740) and Cornhill (1766).
2  English:  see  Cornall  .
3  English:  see  Cornwall  .

variants.name: Cornwall
meanings: English:

habitational name from the county of Cornwall, which is named with the Old English tribal name Cornwealas. This is from Kernow (the term that the Cornish used to refer to themselves, a word of uncertain etymology, perhaps connected with a Celtic element meaning ‘horn, headland’), + Old English wealas ‘strangers, foreigners’, the term used by the Anglo-Saxons for British-speaking people.
variant of   Cornwell  .

variants.name: Parish Registers
meanings: English:  see  Lonsdale  .   Redmonds, Dictionary of Yorks Surnames   notes Tobias Londsdale or Lansdall, 1675–84 in   Parish Registers   (Keighley, WR Yorks).

variants.name: Cornwell
meanings: English:
habitational name from Cornwell in Oxfordshire, named from Old English corn, a metathesized form of cron, cran ‘crane’ + well(a) ‘spring, stream’.

variant of Cornwall or Cornhill; in southeastern England mainly a habitational name from Cornhill in London but elsewhere alternatively from Cornhill (Northumberland) or one of the many minor places so called, often becoming Cornell .

variants.name: Cornell
meanings: English: shortened form of Cornwell , Cornwall , or of Cornhill, a habitational name from a place in Northumberland named Cornhill, from Old English corn, a metathesized form of cron, cran ‘crane’ + halh ‘nook, recess’; or from Cornhill in London, a medieval grain exchange, named with Old English corn ‘corn, grain’ + hyll ‘hill’, or from some other place elsewhere similarly named.
English: variant of   Corney  .
English: possibly also a nickname from French corneille ‘rook, crow’, probably denoting a chatterer or someone with dark hair or a dark complexion.

variants.name: Cornall
meanings: 1 English: the Lancs name is a variant of Corney , where -all and -ell are hypercorrect pronunciations of Cornah, Corner, and Cornoe, late pronunciations of Corney.

2 English: the Devon name is a variant of Cornwall . Here also probably belongs Gurnall, as in Joan Gurnall, 1586 in IGI (Crediton, Devon), and William Gurnall, 1650 in IGI (Lavenham, Suffolk). Compare Corney .

nameMeanings.name: Gervase
meanings: Same as Gervas.

variants.name: Gervais
meanings: Origin uncertain. See Gervaise .

en.lifeSummary: When Gervase de Cornhill was born in 1123, in Cornhill on Tweed, Northumberland, England, his father, Roger de Cornhill, Joint Sheriff of London, was 54 and his mother, Ingenolda de Conteville, was 29. He had at least 4 sons and 1 daughter with Agnes Cornhill. In 1170, his occupation is listed as royal justice. He died in 1183, at the age of 60.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Gervase de Cornhill est né en 1123, à Cornhill on Tweed, Northumberland, Angleterre, son père, Roger de Cornhill, Joint Sheriff of London, avait 54 ans et sa mère, Ingenolda de Conteville, avait 29 ans. Il a eu au moins 4 fils et 1 fille avec Agnes Cornhill. En 1170, sa profession de son indiquée est royal justice. Il est décédé en 1183, à l’âge de 60 ans.

de.Cornhill-Upon-Tweed, Northumberland, Eng: Cornhill on Tweed, Northumberland, England, United Kingdom
de.lifeSummary: Als Gervase de Cornhill 1123 in Cornhill on Tweed, Northumberland, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Roger de Cornhill, Joint Sheriff of London 54 und seine Mutter Ingenolda de Conteville 29. Er hatte mit Agnes Cornhill mindestens 4 Söhne und 1 Tochter. 1170 ist sein Beruf als royal justice aufgeführt. Er starb 1183 im Alter von 60 Jahren.

ja.lifeSummary: Gervase de Cornhillが1123年にイギリスノースウンバーランドコルンヒル オン トウィードで生まれた時，父Roger de Cornhill, Joint Sheriff of Londonは54歳，母Ingenolda de Contevilleは29歳であった。彼はAgnes Cornhillとの間に少なくとも4人の息子と1人の娘をもうけた。1170年，職業はroyal justiceと記録されている。彼は1183年に60歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Gervase de Cornhill(이)가 1123년 영국 노르스벌랜드 코른힐  트위드에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Roger de Cornhill, Joint Sheriff of London(은)는 54세였고, 그의 어머니 Ingenolda de Conteville(은)는 29세였다. 그는 Agnes Cornhill(와)과 적어도 네 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 1170년, 그의 직업은 royal justice이었다. 그는 1183년 60세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.Cornhill-Upon-Tweed, Northumberland, Eng: корнилл он твид, нортумбирланд, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Gervase de Cornhill родился в 1123 г. в местности корнилл он твид, нортумбирланд, Англия, его отцу по имени Roger de Cornhill, Joint Sheriff of London было 54 года, а матери, которую звали Ingenolda de Conteville, 29. У него было по крайней мере 4 сына и 1 дочь от Agnes Cornhill. В 1170 году он работал по профессии royal justice. Он умер в 1183 г. в возрасте 60 лет.
